# GH9904: Product Spec — Rename auto-generated plan titles

## Summary

Allow users to rename an AI plan from the title area in the plan pane header. The custom title should replace the auto-generated title anywhere the plan is shown, restored, synced, or searched, without modifying the rendered markdown plan body.

## Problem

Plan titles are generated by the agent and can become stale, too generic, or hard to recognize after the user edits or reuses a plan. Because plans appear in the plan pane header, conversation artifacts, Warp Drive, plan attachment menus, and search surfaces, a bad title makes it harder to find the right plan later.

## Goals

- Let a user rename a plan directly from the header above the rendered plan content.
- Keep the rename interaction inline and low-friction.
- Persist the user-provided title locally so it survives app restart.
- Sync the user-provided title to Warp Drive for plans that are saved or auto-synced there.
- Use the user-provided title anywhere the plan is listed, attached, previewed, or searched.
- Prevent later agent-generated title updates from overwriting a user-renamed title.
- Preserve the previous title when the user cancels or submits an empty title.

## Non-goals

- Renaming the markdown heading or body content inside the plan.
- Supporting rich text, markdown formatting, or multi-line titles in the title input.
- Adding bulk rename or rename from every plan listing surface.
- Adding a separate reset-to-generated-title flow.
- Changing how the agent generates initial plan titles.
- Changing notebook rename behavior for non-plan Warp Drive notebooks.

## Figma / design references

Figma: none provided.

Maintainer discussion noted that mocks may be useful for the exact title affordance. This spec defines behavior without a mock; if design provides one later, the spec should be updated to match it.

## User experience

### Entry point

- The plan pane header continues to show the current plan title centered above the rendered plan content.
- When the current plan can be renamed, the title area exposes a rename affordance:
  - a pencil icon appears when the title area is hovered or focused, or
  - the title itself can enter inline rename mode through the chosen header interaction.
- The intended primary interaction is inline rename in the header. The final trigger should be resolved by design before implementation:
  - contributor prototype: single-click the title or pencil icon
  - maintainer preference under discussion: double-click the title, similar to a file explorer
- Regardless of the final trigger, users must have a discoverable, keyboard-accessible way to start renaming from the header.

### Rename mode

- Entering rename mode replaces the static title text with a single-line text input in the same header location.
- The input is pre-populated with the current displayed title.
- The full title text is selected when rename mode begins so the user can type a replacement immediately.
- The input remains visually constrained to the header area and should not cause the header controls to jump or obscure the version, sync, share, overflow, or close buttons.
- The rendered markdown plan content remains unchanged and editable according to the existing plan-content editing rules.

### Commit and cancel

- Pressing `Enter` commits the rename.
- Moving focus away from the input commits the rename.
- Pressing `Escape` cancels the rename and restores the previous title.
- Submitting an empty or whitespace-only value is a no-op:
  - the previous title is preserved
  - the user title lock state does not change if no rename actually occurred
  - rename mode exits
- Leading and trailing whitespace are trimmed before saving.
- Re-submitting the same normalized title exits rename mode without creating unnecessary persistence or sync updates.

### Disabled and read-only states

- Rename affordances are hidden or disabled while the plan is still being generated or streamed.
- Rename affordances are hidden or disabled while the user is viewing a previous plan version.
- Rename affordances are hidden or disabled for any read-only/shared-session view where the current user cannot edit the underlying plan.
- If the plan becomes non-renamable while rename mode is open, the edit is canceled rather than committed.

### Persistence and propagation

- After a successful rename, the new title is shown immediately in the plan pane header.
- The title persists in local session restoration. Reopening Warp with the plan pane restored shows the custom title.
- If the plan is saved or auto-synced to Warp Drive, the Warp Drive object title is updated to the custom title.
- Warp Drive list items, previews, command palette search, AI context menu plan attachment results, inline plan menus, and conversation plan artifacts should use the custom title after it is saved.
- If a rename is made before the plan is synced to Warp Drive, the later-created Warp Drive notebook uses the custom title as its initial title.
- If a rename is made after the plan is already synced, the existing Warp Drive notebook title is updated.

### Agent title updates after rename

- Agent-generated titles may continue to set the title before the user has renamed the plan.
- Once a user commits a non-empty custom title, subsequent agent streaming or finalization updates must not overwrite that title.
- Agent updates may continue to update the plan body and versions according to the existing plan behavior.

### Error handling

- Local persistence failures should not block the immediate UI update, but should be logged through existing persistence error paths.
- Warp Drive sync failures should follow existing notebook pending-change and retry behavior.
- The UI should not show a destructive error for an empty title submission because empty input is intentionally a no-op.

## Success criteria

- A user can start renaming from the plan pane header when viewing the current, editable plan.
- The rename input is prefilled, selected, single-line, and commit/cancel behavior is predictable:
  - `Enter` commits
  - blur commits
  - `Escape` cancels
  - empty or whitespace-only input preserves the existing title
- A committed rename updates the visible pane header without changing the rendered markdown body.
- A committed rename survives app restart through local SQLite restoration.
- A committed rename appears in plan listing and search surfaces that currently use plan or notebook title data.
- A committed rename updates the corresponding Warp Drive notebook title when one exists, and becomes the initial notebook title when sync happens later.
- A user-renamed title is not overwritten by later agent title updates for the same plan.
- Rename affordances are unavailable while a plan is streaming, while viewing older versions, or in read-only contexts.
- Existing content editing, version history, plan export, plan attachment, and Warp Drive sync flows continue to work.

## Validation

- Unit-test title normalization and lock behavior in the AI document model:
  - non-empty user rename stores the trimmed title and locks user title
  - empty user rename is ignored
  - agent title update is ignored after lock
  - agent title update still works before lock
- Unit-test local restoration of title and title-lock state.
- Unit-test Warp Drive sync title updates for already-synced plans and initial title creation for newly synced plans.
- Add view or interaction tests where practical for:
  - entering rename mode
  - prefilled selected text
  - `Enter` commit
  - blur commit
  - `Escape` cancel
  - disabled state for streaming and older versions
- Manually verify:
  - renamed plan title appears in the header
  - renamed plan appears in Warp Drive and search results
  - renamed plan can be attached from plan menus
  - app restart restores the renamed title

## Open questions

- Should the primary title trigger be single-click, double-click, pencil-click, or some combination? The contributor prototype uses single-click; maintainer discussion suggested double-click may better match file explorer behavior.
- Should a future reset action allow users to revert to the latest agent-generated title?
- Is there a product-level max title length, or should the implementation rely on existing header clipping and text input behavior?
